title: 10-Year Treasury Yield (DGS10)
summary: The 10-Year Treasury Yield closed at 4.29% on April 8, 2026, down 4 basis points from the prior day's close of 4.33%. The yield has trended slightly lower over the past week, declining from 4.35% on April 3.
key_points: Recent range: 4.29%–4.35% over the past week
why_it_matters: The 10-year Treasury yield is a benchmark rate that influences mortgage costs, corporate borrowing costs, and equity valuations. Current level: 4.29%.
macro_context: Yield at 4.29% as of April 8, 2026, down from 4.35% on April 3. Data reflects market pricing of 10-year Treasury securities.
market_structure_context: FRED DGS10 series tracks daily closing yield on 10-Year constant maturity Treasury securities.
